# subagent-auto-manager TLDR

Only use this as a small supplement to Codex built-in agent tools.

Use it to check counts, states, and agent ids. Do not use it to replace `spawn_agent`, `wait_agent`, `resume_agent`, `close_agent`, or result gathering.

Minimize tool calls. Keep output small. Prefer counts first, then narrow id lists. Avoid broad/history/detail queries unless a human is debugging.

## One Scenario: Many Agents In Waves

```sh
# 1. Quick counts only. No runs.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest

# 2. Launch many agents with Codex built-in spawn_agent.

# 3. Wait for every current running, not-closed agent.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --timeout-ms 600000 --text
# During wait, newly stopped agent ids stream on stderr.

# 4. Get stopped agent ids only.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest --status stopped

# 5. Gather each stopped agent with Codex built-in tools, then close_agent when done.
```

Default no-arg output:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "running": 12,
    "stopped": 0,
    "closed": 0,
    "total": 12,
    "shown": 12
  },
  "runs": []
}
```

Filtered output returns only:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "running": 0,
    "stopped": 12,
    "closed": 0,
    "total": 12,
    "shown": 12
  },
  "runs": [
    { "agentId": "agent-a", "state": "stopped" },
    { "agentId": "agent-b", "state": "stopped" }
  ]
}
```

## States

`running`: `SubagentStart` seen; no `SubagentStop`; not closed.

`stopped`: `SubagentStop` seen, or a later `SubagentStart` for the same `session_id` came from a different hook parent PID after parent shutdown. The subagent turn ended or was made stale. The thread may still be open.

`closed`: successful Codex built-in `close_agent` was seen. `resume_agent` clears this mark.

## Built-In Tool Mapping

```text
spawn_agent       -> SubagentStart             -> running
subagent finishes -> SubagentStop              -> stopped
parent restart    -> SubagentStart parent PID change -> stale running agents become stopped
close_agent       -> PostToolUse close_agent   -> closed
resume_agent      -> PostToolUse resume_agent  -> clears closed
```

PostToolUse tool names accepted:

```text
close_agent
resume_agent
multi_agent_v1close_agent
multi_agent_v1resume_agent
multi_agent_v1__close_agent
multi_agent_v1.resume_agent
```

Do not generalize to `multi_agent_v2...` or `multi_agent_v10...`.

Codex built-in multi-target `wait_agent` may return when any target completes. This package's `wait` is wait-all:

```sh
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --timeout-ms 600000 --text
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --agent <agent-a> --agent <agent-b> --timeout-ms 600000 --text
```

During wait, stopped agents stream to stderr. Final result stays on stdout.

## Reset

```sh
# After gather/close workflow, bulk-hide completed stopped agents.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest reset

# Human debugging only: reopen visibility for one closed agent.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest reset --agent <agent-id> --human
```

Default `reset`: `stopped -> closed`.

`reset --agent ... --human`: clears one `closed` mark.

## Avoid

Do not ask for all states, closed-only lists, historical lists, or full payloads during normal agent work. These create large output and are for human debugging only.

## Hook Setup

Use this command for `SubagentStart`, `SubagentStop`, and `PostToolUse` close/resume hooks:

```sh
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest hook
```

PostToolUse matcher:

```text
(close_agent|resume_agent)$
```

After changing hooks, open `/hooks` in Codex and trust them.

## Storage

```text
<project>/.codex/subagent_auto_manager.db/ledger.sqlite3
```

Hooks use `session_id` and record the hook parent PID. CLI defaults to `CODEX_THREAD_ID`.
